content:
<action>
<tool>graph.plan</tool>
<objective>Populate the novel structure with content based on owner input.</objective>
<constraints>Must adhere to the established directory structure and file templates.</constraints>
<assumptions>The owner will provide the premise and themes.</assumptions>
<risks>Content generation might deviate from the owner's vision if the premise is not clearly defined.</risks>
<steps>1. Request premise and themes from the owner.
2. Populate project/premise.md and project/themes.md.
3. Populate setting/ files (cosmology, factions, locations, society, technology, timeline).
4. Populate character/ files (antagonist, protagonist, relationships, supporting-cast).
5. Populate plot/ files (act-structure, chapter-spine, conflict-lattice).
6. Populate continuity/ files (glossary, open-questions, rules).
7. Populate manuscript/ files (draft-boundary, scene-seeds).
8. Verify content with artifact.audit.</steps>
<checks>1. Does the content align with the premise?
2. Are all required files populated?
3. Does the content follow the established structure?</checks>
<paths>project/premise.md, project/themes.md, setting/cosmology.md, setting/factions.md, setting/locations.md, setting/society.md, setting/technology.md, setting/timeline.md, characters/antagonist.md, characters/protagonist.md, characters/relationships.md, characters/supporting-cast.md, plot/act-structure.md, plot/chapter-spine.md, plot/conflict-lattice.md, continuity/glossary.md, continuity/open-questions.md, continuity/rules.md, manuscript/draft-boundary.md, manuscript/scene-seeds.md</paths>
<reason>The structure is ready, but the content is currently empty templates. A plan is needed to transition from scaffolding to content generation, starting with owner input.</reason>
</graph.plan>
</action>
